nodes.context: Independent military force and criminal organization active in South Vietnam, involved in organized crime and fighting communists.
Communist-led independence coalition in Vietnam; Bình Xuyên initially sided with them.
Religious sect with armed forces in South Vietnam; allied with Bình Xuyên in 1955.
Buddhist sect with armed forces in South Vietnam; allied with Bình Xuyên in 1955.
Criminal groups from Macau that controlled casinos in Saigon before Bảy Viễn took over.

edges.context: Bình Xuyên initially sided with the Việt Minh and fought against the French.
In March 1955, Bình Xuyên joined the Cao Đài and Hòa Hảo in forming a 'United Front of the National Forces.'
In March 1955, Bình Xuyên joined the Cao Đài and Hòa Hảo in forming a 'United Front of the National Forces.'
Bảy Viễn forcibly took control of casinos from Macanese organized crime groups.
